article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
obligation_text: Deployers shall assign human oversight to natural persons who have the necessary competence, training and authority, as well as the necessary support.
scoring_text: Skill in designing human oversight assignments for high-risk AI systems, including competency assessment of oversight personnel, training program design, authority delegation frameworks, and support structure provision.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in designing human oversight assignments for high-risk AI systems, including competency assessment of oversight personnel, training program design, authority delegation frameworks, and support structure provision.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing human oversight mechanisms for AI-assisted decision-making, including escalation triggers, override procedures, human-in-the-loop protocols, and meaningful human control verification.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 26(2) (competency) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-007 is the strongest match (STS: 0.666, cosine: 0.789). Secondary: AG-K-002 (AG, cosine: 0.727), AG-K-007 (AG, cosine: 0.725).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.7/10 (raw 0.666). Shared vocabulary: [ai, designing, human, oversight, skill]. EU AI Act competency requirement terms [assessment, assignments, authority, competency, delegation, design] vs WIDAI terms [assisted, control, decision, escalation, loop, making] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.905 (P=0.898, R=0.911). Balanced P/R — the 26-word EU AI Act competency and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.7%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.789 > STS 0.666 (gap +0.123). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, designing, human, oversight, skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 22.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, designing, human, oversight, skill]. EU AI Act-only: [assessment, assignments, authority, competency, delegation, design]. WIDAI-only: [assisted, control, decision, escalation, loop, making].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.123 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
